guidelines.title: Anticoagulation Therapy Dosing Guidelines
guidelines.content:
ANTICOAGULATION THERAPY DOSING GUIDELINES

WARFARIN (COUMADIN):
- Initial dose: 5 mg daily for most patients
- Elderly/low body weight: Start 2.5 mg daily
- Target INR: 2.0-3.0 for atrial fibrillation, DVT/PE
- Target INR: 2.5-3.5 for mechanical heart valves
- Dose adjustments: Based on INR response
- Loading dose NOT recommended due to bleeding risk

HEPARIN (UNFRACTIONATED):
- VTE treatment: 80 units/kg IV bolus, then 18 units/kg/hour infusion
- ACS: 60 units/kg bolus (max 4000 units), then 12 units/kg/hour (max 1000 units/hour)
- Target aPTT: 1.5-2.5 times control (60-80 seconds)
- Adjust by 2-4 units/kg/hour based on aPTT

ENOXAPARIN (LOVENOX):
- DVT/PE treatment: 1 mg/kg SC every 12 hours OR 1.5 mg/kg SC once daily
- DVT prophylaxis: 40 mg SC once daily
- ACS (NSTEMI/UA): 1 mg/kg SC every 12 hours
- Renal adjustment (CrCl <30): 1 mg/kg SC once daily for treatment
- Maximum: 100 mg per dose for treatment
- Anti-Xa monitoring for obesity, renal impairment

RIVAROXABAN (XARELTO):
- DVT/PE: 15 mg twice daily with food x21 days, then 20 mg once daily
- Afib: 20 mg once daily with evening meal
- Renal adjustment: CrCl 15-50: 15 mg once daily for afib
- Contraindicated: CrCl <15 mL/min

APINXABAN (ELIQUIS):
- Afib: 5 mg twice daily
- Afib reduced dose: 2.5 mg twice daily if 2+ of: age ≥80, weight ≤60 kg, Cr ≥1.5
- DVT/PE: 10 mg twice daily x7 days, then 5 mg twice daily
- DVT prophylaxis post-surgery: 2.5 mg twice daily

DABIGATRAN (PRADAXA):
- Afib: 150 mg twice daily
- Afib reduced dose: 75 mg twice daily if CrCl 15-30
- DVT/PE: 150 mg twice daily after 5-10 days parenteral anticoagulation
- Take with food to reduce GI upset
- Contraindicated: CrCl <15 mL/min

EDOXABAN (SAVAYSA):
- Afib: 60 mg once daily
- Reduced dose: 30 mg once daily if CrCl 15-50, weight ≤60 kg, or P-gp inhibitors
- DVT/PE: 60 mg once daily after 5-10 days parenteral anticoagulation
- NOT recommended if CrCl >95 (reduced efficacy)

BRIDGING ANTICOAGULATION:
- Stop warfarin 5 days before procedure
- Start LMWH 3 days before if high-risk
- Last LMWH dose 24 hours before procedure
- Resume warfarin evening of procedure day
- Continue LMWH until INR therapeutic

REVERSAL AGENTS:
- Warfarin: Vitamin K 10 mg IV slow infusion, FFP/PCC for urgent
- Heparin: Protamine 1 mg per 100 units heparin (max 50 mg)
- Enoxaparin: Protamine 1 mg per 1 mg enoxaparin (60% reversal)
- Dabigatran: Idarucizumab (Praxbind) 5 g IV
- Rivaroxaban/Apixaban: Andexanet alfa (Andexxa)

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (ACC/AHA guidelines, CHEST guidelines)

guidelines.title: Antihypertensive Medication Dosing Guidelines
guidelines.content:
ANTIHYPERTENSIVE MEDICATION DOSING GUIDELINES

ACE INHIBITORS:

Lisinopril:
- Initial: 10 mg once daily
- Target: 20-40 mg once daily
- Maximum: 80 mg/day
- Heart failure: Start 2.5-5 mg, target 20-40 mg

Enalapril:
- Initial: 5 mg once or twice daily
- Target: 10-20 mg twice daily
- Maximum: 40 mg/day
- Heart failure: Start 2.5 mg twice daily

Ramipril:
- Initial: 2.5 mg once daily
- Target: 10 mg once daily
- Post-MI: 2.5 mg twice daily, titrate to 5 mg twice daily

ARBs (Angiotensin Receptor Blockers):

Losartan:
- Initial: 50 mg once daily
- Target: 50-100 mg once daily
- Maximum: 100 mg/day
- Start 25 mg if volume depleted

Valsartan:
- Initial: 80-160 mg once daily
- Target: 160-320 mg once daily
- Heart failure: Start 40 mg twice daily, target 160 mg twice daily

Olmesartan:
- Initial: 20 mg once daily
- Target: 20-40 mg once daily
- Maximum: 40 mg/day

CALCIUM CHANNEL BLOCKERS:

Amlodipine:
- Initial: 5 mg once daily
- Target: 5-10 mg once daily
- Elderly: Start 2.5 mg
- Maximum: 10 mg/day

Diltiazem (extended-release):
- Initial: 180 mg once daily
- Target: 180-360 mg once daily
- Maximum: 480 mg/day
- Do NOT crush extended-release

Verapamil (extended-release):
- Initial: 180 mg once daily
- Target: 180-480 mg once daily
- Maximum: 480 mg/day
- Avoid in heart failure with reduced EF

BETA-BLOCKERS:

Metoprolol succinate (Toprol XL):
- Hypertension: 25-100 mg once daily, max 400 mg
- Heart failure: Start 12.5-25 mg daily, target 200 mg daily
- Post-MI: 25-50 mg twice daily (tartrate) for 2 days, then 100 mg twice daily

Carvedilol:
- Hypertension: 6.25 mg twice daily, max 25 mg twice daily
- Heart failure: Start 3.125 mg twice daily, target 25 mg twice daily (50 mg if >85 kg)
- Take with food

Bisoprolol:
- Initial: 5 mg once daily
- Target: 5-20 mg once daily
- Heart failure: Start 1.25 mg daily, target 10 mg daily

DIURETICS:

Hydrochlorothiazide (HCTZ):
- Initial: 12.5-25 mg once daily
- Target: 25-50 mg once daily
- Maximum: 50 mg/day (higher doses don't add BP benefit)

Chlorthalidone:
- Initial: 12.5-25 mg once daily
- Target: 12.5-25 mg once daily
- Maximum: 50 mg/day
- More potent than HCTZ, longer half-life

Furosemide:
- Hypertension: 20-80 mg/day in 1-2 doses
- Heart failure: 20-80 mg IV/PO, max 600 mg/day
- Twice daily dosing for chronic use

Spironolactone:
- Hypertension: 25-50 mg once daily
- Heart failure: 12.5-25 mg once daily
- Resistant hypertension: 25-50 mg once daily
- Monitor potassium closely

BP TARGETS:
- General population: <130/80 mmHg
- Diabetes: <130/80 mmHg
- CKD: <130/80 mmHg
- Elderly (≥65): <130/80 if tolerated, avoid <120 systolic

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (JNC 8, ACC/AHA 2017 guidelines)

guidelines.title: Heart Failure Medication Dosing Guidelines
guidelines.content:
HEART FAILURE WITH REDUCED EJECTION FRACTION (HFrEF) DOSING GUIDELINES

GDAMT (Guideline-Directed Medical Therapy):

ACE INHIBITORS/ARBs:
- See Cardiology-002 for dosing
- Target maximum tolerated doses
- Monitor creatinine and potassium

ARNI (Sacubitril/Valsartan - Entresto):
- Initial: 49/51 mg twice daily (if on ACE-I) or 24/26 mg twice daily (if ACE-I naive, low BP, or renal impairment)
- Target: 97/103 mg twice daily
- 36-hour washout from ACE-I before starting
- Monitor for angioedema, hypotension, hyperkalemia

BETA-BLOCKERS (Evidence-based for HFrEF):
- Carvedilol: Start 3.125 mg BID, target 25 mg BID (50 mg BID if >85 kg)
- Metoprolol succinate: Start 12.5-25 mg daily, target 200 mg daily
- Bisoprolol: Start 1.25 mg daily, target 10 mg daily
- Double dose every 2 weeks as tolerated
- Hold if HR <60 or symptomatic bradycardia

MINERALOCORTICOID RECEPTOR ANTAGONISTS (MRAs):

Spironolactone:
- Initial: 12.5-25 mg once daily
- Target: 25-50 mg once daily
- Avoid if K >5.0 or CrCl <30
- Monitor potassium at 1 week, 1 month, then q3 months

Eplerenone:
- Initial: 25 mg once daily
- Target: 50 mg once daily
- Fewer anti-androgen side effects than spironolactone

SGLT2 INHIBITORS (New pillar of therapy):

Dapagliflozin (Farxiga):
- Dose: 10 mg once daily
- No titration needed
- Can use in diabetic and non-diabetic HFrEF
- Avoid if eGFR <20

Empagliflozin (Jardiance):
- Dose: 10 mg once daily
- Approved for HFrEF regardless of diabetes status
- Monitor for genital mycotic infections, volume depletion

HYDRALAZINE/ISDN (for ACE-I/ARB intolerant or African Americans):

Hydralazine:
- Initial: 25 mg three times daily
- Target: 75 mg three times daily
- Maximum: 300 mg/day

Isosorbide dinitrate:
- Initial: 20 mg three times daily
- Target: 40 mg three times daily
- Maximum: 160 mg/day
- 12-hour nitrate-free interval to prevent tolerance

Fixed combination (BiDil):
- 37.5 mg hydralazine/20 mg ISDN
- Start 1 tablet TID, target 2 tablets TID

IVABRADINE (Corlanor):
- Indication: HR ≥70 on max beta-blocker, NYHA II-III
- Initial: 5 mg twice daily
- Target: 7.5 mg twice daily
- Reduce to 2.5 mg BID if HR <50 or symptomatic bradycardia

DIGOXIN:
- Loading: 0.5 mg IV/PO, then 0.25 mg q6h x2 doses (optional)
- Maintenance: 0.125-0.25 mg once daily
- Target level: 0.5-0.9 ng/mL (not >1.0)
- Reduce dose in renal impairment, elderly
- Avoid hypokalemia (increases toxicity risk)

DIURETICS (Symptom relief, no mortality benefit):

Furosemide:
- Oral: 20-80 mg once or twice daily
- IV: 20-80 mg, may double if inadequate response
- Continuous infusion: 5-10 mg/hour after bolus

Bumetanide:
- 1 mg bumetanide = 40 mg furosemide
- Oral: 0.5-2 mg once or twice daily
- Better oral bioavailability than furosemide in edematous states

Torsemide:
- 20 mg torsemide = 40 mg furosemide
- Oral: 10-20 mg once daily
- Longest half-life, more predictable absorption

Melatoside (Combination with thiazide for diuretic resistance):
- Add metolazone 2.5-5 mg 30 minutes before loop diuretic
- Monitor for severe electrolyte depletion

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (ACC/AHA/HFSA 2022 guidelines)

guidelines.title: Acute Coronary Syndrome Treatment Protocols
guidelines.content:
ACUTE CORONARY SYNDROME (ACS) TREATMENT PROTOCOLS

STEMI (ST-Elevation MI) - Immediate Treatment:

ASPIRIN:
- Loading dose: 325 mg chewed (non-enteric coated)
- Maintenance: 81 mg daily indefinitely

P2Y12 INHIBITORS:

Clopidogrel:
- Loading: 600 mg (or 300 mg if >75 years)
- Maintenance: 75 mg daily
- Duration: 12 months post-PCI

Ticagrelor (Brilinta):
- Loading: 180 mg
- Maintenance: 90 mg twice daily
- More potent, reversible, dyspnea side effect
- Give with aspirin 81 mg only (not 325 mg)

Prasugrel (Effient):
- Loading: 60 mg
- Maintenance: 10 mg daily (5 mg if <60 kg or >75 years)
- Contraindicated if prior stroke/TIA
- Most potent, highest bleeding risk

ANTICOAGULATION:

Unfractionated Heparin:
- Bolus: 60 units/kg (max 4000 units)
- Infusion: 12 units/kg/hour (max 1000 units/hour)
- Target ACT: 250-300 seconds during PCI

Bivalirudin:
- Bolus: 0.75 mg/kg
- Infusion: 1.75 mg/kg/hour during PCI
- Consider post-PCI infusion: 0.25 mg/kg/hour x4 hours

Enoxaparin:
- 0.5 mg/kg IV bolus if PCI
- 1 mg/kg SC q12h for medical management

GP IIb/IIIa INHIBITORS (selective use):

Abciximab (ReoPro):
- Bolus: 0.25 mg/kg IV
- Infusion: 0.125 mcg/kg/min (max 10 mcg/min) x12 hours

Eptifibatide (Integrilin):
- Bolus: 180 mcg/kg IV
- Infusion: 2 mcg/kg/min x18-24 hours
- Renal adjustment: 1 mcg/kg/min if CrCl <50

FIBRINOLYTICS (if PCI not available within 120 min):

Tenecteplase (TNKase):
- <60 kg: 30 mg IV bolus
- 60-69 kg: 35 mg IV bolus
- 70-79 kg: 40 mg IV bolus
- 80-89 kg: 45 mg IV bolus
- ≥90 kg: 50 mg IV bolus
- Half dose if ≥75 years old

Alteplase (tPA):
- 15 mg IV bolus, then 0.75 mg/kg (max 50 mg) over 30 min
- Then 0.5 mg/kg (max 35 mg) over 60 min
- Total max: 100 mg

Reteplase:
- 10 units IV bolus, repeat 10 units at 30 min

NSTEMI/UNSTABLE ANGINA:

- Same antiplatelet therapy as STEMI
- Invasive strategy (cath within 24-72 hours) for high-risk
- Conservative strategy acceptable for low-risk

ADDITIONAL MEDICATIONS:

Beta-Blockers (within 24 hours if no contraindications):
- Metoprolol 25-50 mg PO q6h, then convert to extended-release
- Target HR 50-60 bpm
- Avoid if: HR <60, SBP <100, signs of HF, AV block

ACE Inhibitors (within 24 hours):
- Lisinopril 2.5-5 mg, titrate to 10-20 mg daily
- Start if anterior MI, HF, EF <40%, diabetes

High-Intensity Statin:
- Atorvastatin 80 mg daily OR
- Rosuvastatin 20-40 mg daily
- Start within 24 hours, continue indefinitely

Nitroglycerin:
- SL: 0.4 mg q5min x3 for chest pain
- IV: Start 10-20 mcg/min, titrate by 10 mcg q5min
- Max: 200 mcg/min
- Avoid if SBP <90, RV infarct, PDE5 inhibitor use

Morphine:
- 2-4 mg IV q5-15min for pain unresponsive to nitrates
- Use with caution (associated with worse outcomes in some studies)

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (ACC/AHA STEMI/NSTEMI guidelines)

guidelines.title: Atrial Fibrillation Rate and Rhythm Control
guidelines.content:
ATRIAL FIBRILLATION MANAGEMENT GUIDELINES

RATE CONTROL MEDICATIONS:

Beta-Blockers (First-line):

Metoprolol tartrate:
- IV: 2.5-5 mg over 2 min, repeat q5min (max 15 mg)
- PO: 25-100 mg twice daily

Metoprolol succinate:
- PO: 25-200 mg once daily

Esmolol:
- Loading: 500 mcg/kg over 1 min
- Infusion: 50-200 mcg/kg/min
- Short-acting, ideal for acute rate control

Calcium Channel Blockers (Non-dihydropyridine):

Diltiazem:
- IV: 0.25 mg/kg (avg 20 mg) over 2 min, may repeat 0.35 mg/kg at 15 min
- IV infusion: 5-15 mg/hour
- PO: 120-360 mg daily (extended-release)

Verapamil:
- IV: 2.5-5 mg over 2 min, may repeat 5-10 mg at 15-30 min
- PO: 120-360 mg daily (extended-release)
- Avoid in HFrEF

Digoxin (Second-line, or for HFrEF):
- Loading: 0.5 mg IV, then 0.25 mg q6h x2
- Maintenance: 0.125-0.25 mg daily
- Less effective for rate control during exercise
- Target level: 0.5-0.9 ng/mL

Amiodarone (Rate control in HFrEF or refractory):
- IV loading: 150 mg over 10 min, then 1 mg/min x6h, then 0.5 mg/min x18h
- PO: 400 mg twice daily x1 week, then 200-400 mg daily

Rate Control Target:
- Lenient: HR <110 at rest (RACE II trial)
- Strict: HR <80 at rest if symptomatic

RHYTHM CONTROL (Antiarrhythmics):

Class IC (No structural heart disease):

Flecainide:
- 50-150 mg twice daily
- Pill-in-pocket: 200-300 mg single dose (if <70 kg: 200 mg)
- Requires AV nodal blocking agent
- Contraindicated in CAD, HF, structural heart disease

Propafenone:
- 150-300 mg three times daily
- SR: 225-425 mg twice daily
- Pill-in-pocket: 450-600 mg single dose

Class III:

Amiodarone:
- Loading: 400 mg TID x1 week, 400 mg BID x1 week, 400 mg daily x1 week
- Maintenance: 100-200 mg daily
- Most effective, but significant toxicities
- Monitor: TFTs, LFTs, PFTs, ophthalmology annually

Sotalol:
- 80-160 mg twice daily
- Initiate in hospital with QTc monitoring
- QTc must be <500 ms
- Renal adjustment required

Dofetilide (Tikosyn):
- CrCl >60: 500 mcg twice daily
- CrCl 40-60: 250 mcg twice daily
- CrCl 20-40: 125 mcg twice daily
- CrCl <20: Contraindicated
- Initiate in hospital x3 days

Dronedarone (Multaq):
- 400 mg twice daily with meals
- Contraindicated in permanent AF, NYHA IV HF, recent decompensated HF
- Less effective than amiodarone but safer

CARDIOVERSION:

Electrical:
- Synchronized DC cardioversion
- Biphasic: Start 120-200 J, escalate as needed
- Monophasic: 200 J, escalate to 360 J

Pharmacological:
- Ibutilide: 1 mg IV over 10 min, may repeat once
  - Monitor QTc, risk of torsades
- Flecainide/Propafenone: Pill-in-pocket approach

Anticoagulation for Cardioversion:
- AF >48 hours or unknown duration: 3 weeks anticoagulation before OR TEE to exclude LAA thrombus
- Post-cardioversion: 4 weeks anticoagulation minimum (longer based on CHA2DS2-VASc)

CHA2DS2-VASc SCORING:
- CHF: 1 point
- Hypertension: 1 point
- Age ≥75: 2 points
- Diabetes: 1 point
- Stroke/TIA: 2 points
- Vascular disease: 1 point
- Age 65-74: 1 point
- Sex (female): 1 point

Anticoagulation Recommendations:
- Score 0 (male) or 1 (female): No anticoagulation
- Score 1 (male): Consider anticoagulation
- Score ≥2: Anticoagulation recommended

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (ACC/AHA/HRS AF guidelines)
